growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Methanoperedenaceae archaea: a 20-year research journey. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-69699-0. [PMC13046784]
growth_records.snippet: Anammox bacteria, initially thought to have doubling times exceeding 10 days, have recently been shown to double every 2.1 days
growth_records.notes: Review-cited secondary value (refs 124,125), not a primary measurement of this paper. Doubling time = 2.1 days => td = 2.1*24 = 50.4 h. mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/50.4 = 0.01375 /h. Historical value '>10 days' also mentioned (td>240 h => mu<0.00289 /h).
